---
title: "Madagascar Influenza — Phylogenetic Analysis"
subtitle: "IQ-TREE ML trees · Monophyletic clade analysis"
author: "Camila Duitama"
date: today
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    output-file: "trees.html"
    output-dir: "../docs"
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: "Contents"
    number-sections: true
    theme: cosmo
    fig-width: 10
    fig-height: 6
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

from pathlib import Path
import pandas as pd

RESULTS_DIR = Path("../results")
TREES_DIR   = Path("../trees")
DATA_DIR    = Path("../data")

summary = pd.read_csv(RESULTS_DIR / "clade_summary.tsv", sep="\t")
details = pd.read_csv(RESULTS_DIR / "clade_details.tsv", sep="\t")
metadata = pd.read_csv(DATA_DIR / "combined_metadata.tsv", sep="\t", low_memory=False)

# Subtype → metadata Subtype label
SUBTYPE_MAP = {
    "H3N2": "A / H3N2",
    "H1N1": "A / H1N1",
    "B":    "B",
    "H9N2": "A / H9N2",
    "H6N2": "A / H6N2",
}

def parse_tree_name(name):
    parts = name.split("_", 1)
    return parts[0], parts[1]  # seg, subtype
```

← [Back to main report](index.html)

---

## Pipeline

Up to **16 maximum-likelihood trees** are built across all segment × subtype combinations with ≥ 10 Madagascar and ≥ 20 other African sequences.
All sequences are used — **no subsampling**.

| Category | Segments | Strategy | Trees |
|----------|----------|----------|-------|
| HA per-subtype | HA | One tree per HA subtype | up to 5 |
| NA per-subtype | NA | One tree per NA subtype | up to 5 |
| Internal merged | PB2, PB1, PA, NP, MP, NS | All subtypes merged per segment | 6 |
| **Total** | | | **up to 16** |

**Tree naming:** each tree is named `<segment>_<subtype>`. For example, `HA_B` means the **Hemagglutinin (HA) gene segment** of **Influenza B** viruses; `NA_H3N2` means the **Neuraminidase (NA) gene segment** of **H3N2** viruses. HA and NA are the two surface proteins that determine subtype (the H and N numbers) and are the main targets of the immune response.

### IQ-TREE parameters

Each tree was built with **IQ-TREE 3.1.0** using the following command:

```bash
iqtree3 -s <alignment.fasta> \
        -m HKY+G          \ # substitution model: HKY (transitions ≠ transversions) + Gamma rate variation
        -B 1000           \ # ultrafast bootstrap (UFBoot2): 1000 replicates, values shown as node labels
        -T <N_CPUs>       \ # multi-threading
        --seed 42         \ # reproducibility
        --prefix <NAME>   \ # output prefix
        --redo               # overwrite existing run
```

| Parameter | Value | Meaning |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| `-m HKY+G` | HKY + Gamma | 2-parameter nucleotide model (κ for ts/tv ratio) with 4 discrete rate categories |
| `-B 1000` | UFBoot2 | Ultrafast bootstrap approximation; node support values are bootstrap percentages |
| `--seed 42` | 42 | Fixed random seed for reproducibility |

**Branch support:** node labels are UFBoot2 percentages (0–100). Values ≥ 95 are considered strongly supported.

**No subsampling:** all Africa sequences (Madagascar + rest of Africa) with the relevant segment enter the alignment directly. IQ-TREE internally deduplicates identical sequences for the ML optimisation, but adds them back as tips in the final tree — so **every input sequence appears in the treefile**.

### Tree rooting

IQ-TREE outputs unrooted trees (no `-o` outgroup was specified). Before any clade detection, each tree is **midpoint-rooted**: the root is placed at the midpoint of the longest tip-to-tip path in the tree. This is implemented via ete3's `get_midpoint_outgroup()` / `set_outgroup()` and is a standard approach when no outgroup sequence is available.

### Clade detection

Before looking for Madagascar-only groups, every tree branch with UFBoot2 support **< 70** is collapsed.

A **monophyletic Madagascar clade** is then defined as the largest group of tree tips (≥ 2) that are all exclusively from Madagascar and share a common ancestor not shared with any non-Madagascar sequence.

## Statistics computed per tree

| Metric | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| `n_mdg_clades` | Number of monophyletic Madagascar clades (≥ 2 tips) |
| `mdg_pd_fraction` | What fraction of the tree's total branch length is "owned" by Madagascar sequences (see below) |
| `mrca_subtree_fraction` | How much of the tree sits inside the subtree that spans all Madagascar sequences (see below) |

**`mdg_pd_fraction` — how much of the tree's evolutionary space do Madagascar sequences cover?**

Imagine the tree as a road map: every branch is a road segment with a length equal to the number of mutations along that branch. Faith's Phylogenetic Diversity (PD) for Madagascar is the total length of all roads you need to drive to visit every Madagascar sequence starting from the root. If Madagascar sequences are scattered all over the tree, you need to drive almost the entire map — high PD fraction. If they all cluster in one corner, you only need a short detour — low PD fraction. This metric is divided by the total map length so it becomes a percentage of the whole tree.

**`mrca_subtree_fraction` — how spread out are Madagascar sequences across the tree?**

The **MRCA (Most Recent Common Ancestor)** of all Madagascar sequences is the single node in the tree that is the common ancestor of every Madagascar tip. It is essentially the point where all Madagascar lineages diverge. The subtree *below* that node contains everything nested between Madagascar sequences — including non-Madagascar sequences that happen to sit among them. `mrca_subtree_fraction` is the branch-length sum of that whole subtree divided by the total tree length.

- **Value ≈ 1.0** (e.g., Flu B, H3N2): The MRCA is near the root, meaning Madagascar sequences span almost the entire tree. This happens when Madagascar has sequences in many different global lineages — consistent with frequent re-importation every season.
- **Value ≈ 0.03** (e.g., NA_H9N2): The MRCA subtree covers only 3% of the tree. All Madagascar sequences are bunched together in a tiny corner — consistent with a single introduction that stayed isolated.

## Statistics computed per clade

| Metric | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| `internal_bl` | Sum of branch lengths within the clade — within-Madagascar diversity |
| `stem_bl` | Branch from clade root to parent — divergence from nearest African ancestor |
| `clade_prop_tree_bl` | `internal_bl` ÷ total tree branch length — clade's share of total tree diversity |

::: {.callout-note}
Results are based on **10 HA/NA trees** (full Africa sequences, no subsampling, UFBoot2 1000 replicates).
Internal segment trees (6 segments) are pending.
:::

---

## Input data coverage

For each tree, **every** Africa sequence with the relevant segment was used — no subsampling.
IQ-TREE removes sequences that are 100% identical during the ML tree search (to save computation), but adds them back as tips in the final tree — so the treefile contains all input sequences and no sequence is truly excluded.
The difference between GISAID isolate counts and tree tips reflects isolates that exist in GISAID but for which the relevant gene segment (HA or NA) was never sequenced or was not included in the GISAID download.

```{python}
#| label: tbl-coverage
#| tbl-cap: "Input sequences per tree. *Tips in tree* = all sequences provided as input (IQ-TREE re-attaches identical sequences at the end, so none are lost). *GISAID isolates* = total Africa isolates with this subtype in metadata. *Missing segment* = isolates in GISAID that do not have this gene segment sequenced."

rows = []
for _, row in summary.iterrows():
    name    = row["tree"]
    n_mdg   = int(row["n_mdg_tips"])
    n_afr   = int(row["n_africa_tips"])
    n_tips  = n_mdg + n_afr

    seg, sub = parse_tree_name(name)
    meta_label = SUBTYPE_MAP.get(sub)
    n_gisaid = int((metadata["Subtype"] == meta_label).sum()) if meta_label else None
    no_segment = (n_gisaid - n_tips) if n_gisaid else None
    pct_missing = (100 * no_segment / n_gisaid) if (n_gisaid and no_segment is not None) else None

    rows.append({
        "Tree":              name,
        "Tips in tree":     f"{n_tips:,}",
        "Madagascar":       f"{n_mdg:,}",
        "Other Africa":     f"{n_afr:,}",
        "% Madagascar":     f"{100*n_mdg/n_tips:.1f}%",
        "GISAID isolates¹": f"{n_gisaid:,}" if n_gisaid else "—",
        "Missing segment²": f"{no_segment:,}" if no_segment is not None else "—",
        "% missing²":       f"{pct_missing:.1f}%" if pct_missing is not None else "—",
    })

cov = pd.DataFrame(rows).set_index("Tree")
cov.style.set_properties(**{"text-align": "right"}).set_table_styles(
    [{"selector": "th", "props": [("text-align", "center")]}]
)
```
¹ Total GISAID Africa isolates with this subtype (all hosts, from `combined_metadata.tsv`).  
² Isolates in GISAID that do not have the relevant gene segment (HA or NA) sequenced; these were not part of any tree.

```{python}
#| output: asis
n_total = summary["n_mdg_tips"] + summary["n_africa_tips"]
mdg_pct = 100 * summary["n_mdg_tips"] / n_total
print(f"*Trees range from {int(n_total.min()):,} ({summary.loc[n_total.idxmin(), 'tree']}) to {int(n_total.max()):,} ({summary.loc[n_total.idxmax(), 'tree']}) sequences; Madagascar sequences account for {mdg_pct.min():.1f}–{mdg_pct.max():.1f}% of each tree.*")
```

---

## Clade analysis results

### Summary per tree

```{python}
#| label: tbl-summary
#| tbl-cap: "Per-tree clade statistics. Only groupings with at least 70% bootstrap support were counted as clades; weaker groupings were treated as unresolved. **Mdg seqs**: total Madagascar sequences in the tree. **Africa seqs**: total non-Madagascar African sequences. **Clades**: number of monophyletic Madagascar-only groups (≥ 2 sequences). **Singletons**: Madagascar sequences not assigned to any clade. **% Mdg in clades**: percentage of Madagascar sequences that fall inside a clade. **Mdg PD %**: fraction of total tree phylogenetic diversity attributable to Madagascar sequences. **MRCA frac**: fraction of the tree spanned by the subtree rooted at the most recent common ancestor of all Madagascar sequences (1 = spans the whole tree). **Mean clade size**: average number of Madagascar sequences per clade. **Mean stem BL**: average length of the branch leading into each Madagascar clade (longer = more diverged from non-Madagascar relatives)."

disp = summary[[
    "tree", "n_mdg_tips", "n_africa_tips", "n_mdg_clades",
    "n_mdg_singletons", "pct_mdg_in_clades",
    "mdg_pd_fraction", "mrca_subtree_fraction",
    "mean_clade_size", "mean_stem_bl"
]].copy()

disp.columns = [
    "Tree", "Mdg seqs", "Africa seqs", "Clades",
    "Singletons", "% Mdg in clades",
    "Mdg PD %", "MRCA frac",
    "Mean clade size", "Mean stem BL"
]

# Numeric columns for mean/std (before formatting)
num_cols = ["Mdg seqs", "Africa seqs", "Clades", "Singletons",
            "% Mdg in clades", "Mdg PD %", "MRCA frac",
            "Mean clade size", "Mean stem BL"]

import pandas as pd
means = disp[num_cols].mean()
stds  = disp[num_cols].std()

# Format display columns
disp["Mdg PD %"]        = (disp["Mdg PD %"]  * 100).round(1)
disp["% Mdg in clades"] = disp["% Mdg in clades"].round(1)
disp["MRCA frac"]       = disp["MRCA frac"].round(3)
disp["Mean stem BL"]    = disp["Mean stem BL"].round(4)
disp["Mean clade size"] = disp["Mean clade size"].round(1)

# Build mean ± SD summary row
def fmt_mean_sd(col, mean_val, std_val):
    if col in ("Mdg PD %", "% Mdg in clades"):
        m = mean_val * 100 if col == "Mdg PD %" else mean_val
        s = std_val  * 100 if col == "Mdg PD %" else std_val
        return f"{m:.1f} ± {s:.1f}%"
    if col == "MRCA frac":
        return f"{mean_val:.3f} ± {std_val:.3f}"
    if col == "Mean stem BL":
        return f"{mean_val:.4f} ± {std_val:.4f}"
    return f"{mean_val:.1f} ± {std_val:.1f}"

summary_row = {"Tree": "**Mean ± SD**"}
for col in num_cols:
    summary_row[col] = fmt_mean_sd(col, means[col], stds[col])

# Format display table
disp["Mdg PD %"]        = disp["Mdg PD %"].map("{:.1f}%".format)
disp["% Mdg in clades"] = disp["% Mdg in clades"].map("{:.1f}%".format)
disp["MRCA frac"]       = disp["MRCA frac"].map("{:.3f}".format)
disp["Mean stem BL"]    = disp["Mean stem BL"].map("{:.4f}".format)
disp["Mean clade size"] = disp["Mean clade size"].map("{:.1f}".format)
disp["Mdg seqs"]        = disp["Mdg seqs"].astype(str)
disp["Africa seqs"]     = disp["Africa seqs"].astype(str)
disp["Clades"]          = disp["Clades"].astype(str)
disp["Singletons"]      = disp["Singletons"].astype(str)

disp_full = pd.concat([disp, pd.DataFrame([summary_row])], ignore_index=True)
disp_full.set_index("Tree").style
```

```{python}
#| output: asis
_mrca = summary["mrca_subtree_fraction"]
_clades = summary["n_mdg_clades"]
_pct = summary["pct_mdg_in_clades"]
print(f"*Across the 10 trees, Madagascar sequences form {_clades.mean():.1f} \u00b1 {_clades.std():.1f} clades per tree on average, grouping {_pct.mean():.0f}% of sequences; MRCA fraction ranges from {_mrca.min():.3f} ({summary.loc[_mrca.idxmin(), 'tree']}) to {_mrca.max():.3f} ({summary.loc[_mrca.idxmax(), 'tree']}), spanning tight local clustering to broad seasonal spread.*")
```

### Top clades per tree

```{python}
#| label: tbl-top-clades
#| tbl-cap: "Per-tree mean ± SD across the five largest monophyletic Madagascar clades. **Tips**: number of Madagascar sequences in the clade. **Internal BL**: sum of all branch lengths within the clade (total evolutionary distance among clade members). **Stem BL**: length of the branch leading into the clade from the rest of the tree (longer = more diverged from non-Madagascar relatives). **% Tree BL**: percentage of the whole tree's total branch length that this clade accounts for."

import pandas as pd

top5 = (
    details
    .sort_values(["tree", "n_tips"], ascending=[True, False])
    .groupby("tree")
    .head(5)
    .copy()
)
top5["clade_prop_tree_bl"] = top5["clade_prop_tree_bl"] * 100

col_map = {
    "n_tips": "Tips",
    "internal_bl": "Internal BL",
    "stem_bl": "Stem BL",
    "clade_prop_tree_bl": "% Tree BL"
}

def fmt_t3(mean_val, std_val, col):
    if col == "clade_prop_tree_bl":
        return f"{mean_val:.2f} ± {std_val:.2f}%"
    if col in ("internal_bl", "stem_bl"):
        return f"{mean_val:.4f} ± {std_val:.4f}"
    return f"{mean_val:.1f} ± {std_val:.1f}"

rows = []
for tree, grp in top5.groupby("tree"):
    row = {"Tree": tree}
    for col, label in col_map.items():
        row[label] = fmt_t3(grp[col].mean(), grp[col].std(ddof=1), col)
    rows.append(row)

pd.DataFrame(rows).set_index("Tree").style
```

```{python}
#| output: asis
_top5_mean = top5.groupby("tree")["n_tips"].mean()
_largest = _top5_mean.idxmax()
print(f"*{_largest} has the largest top clades on average ({_top5_mean[_largest]:.0f} Madagascar sequences per clade); across all trees the five largest clades contain {top5['n_tips'].mean():.1f} \u00b1 {top5['n_tips'].std():.1f} sequences on average.*")
```

## Tree visualisation

### Subsampling for visualisation

Full trees can contain thousands of sequences, making them impossible to read visually. To produce viewable trees, we subsampled each tree keeping **all Madagascar sequences** and selecting up to **150 non-Madagascar sequences** using [Treemmer](https://github.com/fmenardo/Treemmer) (Menardo et al. 2018, *BMC Bioinformatics* 19:164).

Each tree was reduced using the following command:

```bash
python Treemmer_v0.3.py <tree.nwk> -X <target> -lm protected.csv -mc <n_mdg> -np -c 4
```

where `-X` sets the target number of tips, `-lm protected.csv` is a file listing all Madagascar tip names with the tag `Madagascar`, and `-mc <n_mdg>` forces Treemmer to always keep at least that many Madagascar-tagged sequences (equal to the total number of Madagascar tips, so none can be removed). All 10 subsampled trees were annotated with tip colours (Madagascar = red, other Africa = grey) and readable tip labels (Madagascar sequences only) for upload to [iTOL](https://itol.embl.de/).

The table below summarises the composition of each subsampled tree:

| Tree | Original tips | Madagascar tips | Non-Madagascar tips kept | Total after subsampling |
|------|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| HA_B | 5,015 | 365 | 150 | 515 |
| HA_H1N1 | 8,621 | 234 | 150 | 384 |
| HA_H3N2 | 9,587 | 375 | 150 | 525 |
| HA_H6N2 | 64 | 27 | 37 | 64 *(full tree, below target)* |
| HA_H9N2 | 1,268 | 51 | 150 | 201 |
| NA_B | 4,292 | 258 | 150 | 408 |
| NA_H1N1 | 7,186 | 203 | 150 | 353 |
| NA_H3N2 | 8,114 | 369 | 150 | 519 |
| NA_H6N2 | 65 | 28 | 37 | 65 *(full tree, below target)* |
| NA_H9N2 | 1,456 | 53 | 150 | 203 |

### Trees selected for display

Two representative trees are shown below; all 10 subsampled trees are available for upload to iTOL.

```{python}
#| label: fig-na-h9n2
#| fig-cap: "NA segment of H9N2 (203 tips: 53 Madagascar in red, 150 other Africa in grey). Madagascar sequences form a single deep-branching clade, consistent with an isolated avian reservoir with no ongoing exchange with the continent."
from IPython.display import SVG, display
display(SVG(filename="figures/NA_H9N2_sub203.svg"))
```

*All 53 Madagascar H9N2 sequences cluster into a single deep-branching clade, separated from other African lineages by long branches — consistent with an isolated avian reservoir with no detected ongoing exchange with the continent.*

```{python}
#| label: fig-ha-h3n2
#| fig-cap: "HA segment of H3N2 (525 tips: 375 Madagascar in red, 150 other Africa in grey). Madagascar sequences are distributed throughout the tree with no dominant clade, consistent with repeated seasonal introductions from the global pool."
display(SVG(filename="figures/HA_H3N2_sub525.svg"))
```

*Madagascar H3N2 sequences (red) are spread throughout all major clades with no dominant cluster, consistent with repeated seasonal reintroduction from the global influenza pool each year.*

---

### ete3 circular plots

Trees are plotted with **ete3** in circular mode. Branch lengths are **log-transformed** (`log(1 + d/m)` where `m` is the median non-zero branch length across all branches) before rendering so that both short and long branches are visible in the same image — the absolute scale is not meaningful, only the relative spread matters. **Madagascar sequences** are shown in red; **other Africa** in grey. All trees are midpoint-rooted.

For each tree, two versions are shown: the **subsampled tree** (all Madagascar + up to 150 other Africa, used for iTOL upload) and the **full tree** (all Africa sequences used for IQ-TREE). HA_H6N2 and NA_H6N2 had fewer tips than the subsample target, so their subsampled and full trees are identical.

```{python}
#| label: ete3-setup
#| include: false
import os, numpy as np
os.environ["QT_QPA_PLATFORM"] = "offscreen"

from pathlib import Path
from ete3 import Tree, TreeStyle, NodeStyle
from IPython.display import Image, display as ipy_display

TREES_DIR = Path("../trees")
VIZ_DIR   = Path("../viz")
FIG_DIR   = Path("figures")
FIG_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

MDG_COLOR   = "#c0392b"
OTHER_COLOR = "#bbbbbb"

def _log_transform(tree):
    dists = [n.dist for n in tree.traverse() if n.dist > 0]
    if not dists:
        return
    scale = float(np.median(dists))
    for n in tree.traverse():
        n.dist = float(np.log1p(n.dist / scale))

def plot_tree_ete3(treefile, outpath, size=900):
    outpath = Path(outpath)
    if not outpath.exists():
        t = Tree(str(treefile), format=1)
        og = t.get_midpoint_outgroup()
        if og is not None:
            t.set_outgroup(og)
        _log_transform(t)
        for node in t.traverse():
            ns = NodeStyle()
            ns["size"] = 0
            is_mdg = node.is_leaf() and "Madagascar" in node.name
            col = MDG_COLOR if is_mdg else OTHER_COLOR
            w   = 2 if is_mdg else 1
            ns["hz_line_color"] = col
            ns["vt_line_color"] = col
            ns["hz_line_width"] = w
            ns["vt_line_width"] = w
            node.set_style(ns)
        ts = TreeStyle()
        ts.mode = "c"
        ts.show_leaf_name    = False
        ts.show_scale        = False
        ts.show_branch_length = False
        ts.show_branch_support = False
        t.render(str(outpath), tree_style=ts, w=size, units="px")
    ipy_display(Image(str(outpath)))
```

#### HA_H6N2

```{python}
#| label: fig-ete3-ha-h6n2-sub
#| fig-cap: "HA_H6N2 — subsampled (64 tips: 27 Madagascar in red, 37 other Africa in grey). Same as the full tree since this subtype had fewer than the subsample target."
plot_tree_ete3(VIZ_DIR / "HA_H6N2_sub64.nwk",
               FIG_DIR / "ete3_HA_H6N2_sub64.png", size=700)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-ete3-ha-h6n2-full
#| fig-cap: "HA_H6N2 — full tree (64 tips: 27 Madagascar in red, 37 other Africa in grey). Identical to the subsampled tree."
plot_tree_ete3(TREES_DIR / "HA_H6N2.treefile",
               FIG_DIR / "ete3_HA_H6N2_full64.png", size=700)
```

#### HA_B (Influenza B)

```{python}
#| label: fig-ete3-ha-b-sub
#| fig-cap: "HA_B — subsampled (515 tips: 365 Madagascar in red, 150 other Africa in grey)."
plot_tree_ete3(VIZ_DIR / "HA_B_sub515.nwk",
               FIG_DIR / "ete3_HA_B_sub515.png", size=1000)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-ete3-ha-b-full
#| fig-cap: "HA_B — full tree (5,015 tips: 365 Madagascar in red, 4,650 other Africa in grey)."
plot_tree_ete3(TREES_DIR / "HA_B.treefile",
               FIG_DIR / "ete3_HA_B_full5015.png", size=1200)
```

#### HA_H9N2

```{python}
#| label: fig-ete3-ha-h9n2-sub
#| fig-cap: "HA_H9N2 — subsampled (201 tips: 51 Madagascar in red, 150 other Africa in grey)."
plot_tree_ete3(VIZ_DIR / "HA_H9N2_sub201.nwk",
               FIG_DIR / "ete3_HA_H9N2_sub201.png", size=800)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-ete3-ha-h9n2-full
#| fig-cap: "HA_H9N2 — full tree (1,268 tips: 51 Madagascar in red, 1,217 other Africa in grey)."
plot_tree_ete3(TREES_DIR / "HA_H9N2.treefile",
               FIG_DIR / "ete3_HA_H9N2_full1268.png", size=1000)
```

#### NA_H6N2

```{python}
#| label: fig-ete3-na-h6n2-sub
#| fig-cap: "NA_H6N2 — subsampled (65 tips: 28 Madagascar in red, 37 other Africa in grey). Same as the full tree."
plot_tree_ete3(VIZ_DIR / "NA_H6N2_sub65.nwk",
               FIG_DIR / "ete3_NA_H6N2_sub65.png", size=700)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-ete3-na-h6n2-full
#| fig-cap: "NA_H6N2 — full tree (65 tips: 28 Madagascar in red, 37 other Africa in grey). Identical to the subsampled tree."
plot_tree_ete3(TREES_DIR / "NA_H6N2.treefile",
               FIG_DIR / "ete3_NA_H6N2_full65.png", size=700)
```
